channel.title: Blog on Nimesh Ghelani
channel.description: Recent content in Blog on Nimesh Ghelani

item.title: vim-nano now supports vim8
item.description: Try out vim-nano I decided to try Tinder one weekend and after no matches, I started wondering if it was universe's way of telling me to attend to a far greater cause. It was then that I realized, vim-nano only supports neovim. April 1st, what a great day to announce that vim-nano now supports vim8!! Back when I initially wrote vim-nano, I relied on the terminal support of neovim to simulate the top and bottom bar of GNU Nano.

item.title: SIGIR 2018
item.description: There was a lot to learn and experience this year at SIGIR. Ann Arbor is not the most exciting place, but certain parts of the UMichigan campus has its charm (plus the fireflies). UMichigan Law Residence Here is my talk at the Professional Search workshop (which btw, was also a great experience). Apart from the alignment issues of my pdf presentation (apparently rendering pdf presentations is still not universally simple), I didn't majestically mess up.

item.title: Using statistics to find whether DoTA Plus is pay2win
item.description: TL;DR No. Note: I did this study few months ago, got bored of it and forgot about it. Most of the writeup here is what I wrote back then. Context (skippable) Few months ago, Valve introduced Dota Plus, a Dota 2 subscription service which replaces the traditional battle passes for Majors. Among many other things, the service offers the dota plus assistant, which is an in-game helper providing players individual stats and on the fly suggestions about items and abilities.
